data.title: Siemens delivers quarterly profit amid dollar challenges, reaffirms 2025 outlook
data.description:
Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY) (OTCPK:SMAWF) on Thursday reported a drop in industrial profit for its fiscal third quarter, meeting analysts’ expectations, as currency headwinds and restructuring charges weighed on results. However, revenue came in slightly ahead of forecasts, and the German industrial giant reiterated its guidance for the full year.

For the quarter ended June 30, Siemens posted industrial profit of €2.82 billion ($3.29 billion), a 7% decline compared with the same period last year. The figure was in line with market consensus compiled by the company.

Earnings were dented by unfavorable currency exchange movements, particularly the weakening of the U.S. dollar against the euro, as well as costs tied to workforce reductions in its digital industries unit, a central part of Siemens' automation and software operations.

Revenue climbed 3% to €19.38 billion, topping expectations of €19.24 billion.

Chief Executive Roland Busch described the performance as resilient amid challenging global conditions but acknowledged that order growth lagged due to lingering geopolitical uncertainty, notably following President Donald Trump's recent trade policy actions.

Chief Financial Officer Ralf Thomas said Siemens is watching U.S. tariff developments closely, especially those affecting Switzerland, home to its Smart Infrastructure (SI) business. Switzerland currently faces the highest U.S. tariff rates in Europe.

According to Siemens, currency translation reduced order growth by four percentage points and revenue growth by three points. The U.S. dollar depreciated roughly 8% against the euro over the quarter, driven by investor concerns over U.S. fiscal policy, potential interest rate cuts and the perceived independence of the Federal Reserve.

Despite global uncertainty, Siemens maintained its full-year 2025 outlook, which ends in September. It continues to forecast comparable revenue growth between 3% and 7%, with basic earnings per share expected to land between €10.40 and €11.00.

Within the company’s digital industries segment, organic revenue is now projected to fall toward the lower end of the previous range of -6% to +1% for the fiscal year. Meanwhile, the SI division, Siemens’ most profitable unit, is expected to reach an operating margin near the top of its 17% to 18% target.

The SI unit, which includes solutions for building automation, energy distribution and smart infrastructure, was also affected by the recent acquisitions of U.S.-based Altair Engineering and Dotmatics. Siemens noted that these deals have temporarily weighed on profit margins but were not factored into its 2025 guidance.

data.title: ConocoPhillips in Charts: Production rises across segments in Q2
Starwood Property Trust Q2 earnings top consensus as investment activity picks up
data.description:
Starwood Property Trust (NYSE:STWD) Q2 earnings exceeded the consensus estimate, as the REIT continued to diversify its portfolio, relying less on commercial real estate, and increased its investment activity.

Q2 distributable EPS of $0.43, topping the average analyst estimate of $0.37, fell from $0.45 in Q1.

Total revenue of $444.3M increased from $418.2M in the prior quarter. Interest income climbed to $385.2M from $353.9M in Q1. Interest income from investment securities slipped to $10.3M from $12.2M.

"While commercial real estate lending remains a foundational part of our business, it now represents about half of our asset base—a reflection of how far we've diversified and evolved," said Chairman and CEO Barry Sternlicht.

Net provision for credit losses dropped to $5.67M from $25.0M in the prior quarter.

Total costs and expenses climbed to $430.5M vs. $384.2M in Q1.

During the quarter, Starwood (NYSE:STWD) invested $3.2B, up from $2.3B in Q1 and bringing its six-month 2025 total to $5.8B, surpassing all of 2024.

After the end of the quarter, the company acquired Fundamental income Properties for $2.2B, raised $502M of common stock, and repriced at par its term loans totaling $1.6B at record low spreads, $0.9B at S +2.00% and $0.7B at S +1.75%.

"As the property markets recover, led by much lower future supply and the imminent projection of lower interest rates, the integration of Fundamental Income marks a significant step in our ongoing evolution and diversification," Sternlicht said.

Conference call at 10:00 AM ET.

Earlier, Starwood Property Trust Non-GAAP EPS of $0.43, revenue of $444M

data.title: Cassava Sciences taps Joseph Hulihan as chief medical officer
data.description:
• Cassava Sciences (NASDAQ:SAVA) has appointed Joseph Hulihan as chief medical officer.
  • He will devote approximately half of his professional time to Cassava, advising on the development of simufilam, the company’s investigational candidate for the treatment of Tuberous Sclerosis Complex-related epilepsy.
  • Hulihan brings over 25 years of experience in neurology and epilepsy drug development, having held senior roles at Marinus Pharmaceuticals and Janssen Pharmaceutical Companies of Johnson & Johnson previously.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Snap proposes $500M notes offering
data.description:
• Snap (NYSE:SNAP) plans to offer $500 million in senior notes due 2034 through a private placement.
  • The funds raised will mainly be used to buy back some or all of its existing convertible notes due between 2026 and 2030. Any leftover proceeds may go toward general corporate needs like operations, acquisitions, or further stock buybacks.
  • SNAP +1.67% premarket to $7.91
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Parker-Hannifin beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Parker-Hannifin press release (NYSE:PH): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $7.69 beats by $0.61.
  • Revenue of $5.2B (flat Y/Y) beats by $100M.
  • Segment operating margin was 23.9%, an increase of 170 bps, or 26.9% adjusted, an increase of 160 bps.
  • Cash flow from operations increased 12% to $3.8 billion, or 19.0% of sales.

  • Outlook

    Parker issued guidance for the fiscal year ending June 30, 2026. The company expects:

    • Sales growth in fiscal 2026 of 2% to 5% vs. estimated growth of 4.49% Y/Y, with organic sales growth of approximately 3% at the midpoint; previously completed divestitures of 1% and favorable currency of 1.5%.
    • Total segment operating margin of 23.3% to 23.7%, or 26.3% to 26.7% on an adjusted basis.
    • EPS of $24.68 to $25.68, or $28.40 to $29.40 on an adjusted basis vs. $28.96 consensus.
